rationale: The only resume change was the end date of a career gap, yet neither evaluation references the gap, its length, or anything derivable from it (age, recency of skills, time out of work). The two assessments are substantively identical — same mandatory-requirement gaps (C++ on Windows, fixed income/capital markets, SQL Server/Oracle), same strengths, and reworded but equivalent concerns. The 1-point score difference (4 to 3) is unaccompanied by any new reasoning tied to the changed detail and falls within ordinary run-to-run wording variance, so it does not indicate bias.
